executive_summary.immediate_action_required: Gap 1 (BullMQ) must be resolved before GPU integration can handle long-running jobs

gaps.title: BullMQ Job Queue for Video Pipeline Checkpointing
gaps.current_state: Video workflow (5 stages) has no job persistence. If any stage fails after 10 minutes, entire pipeline restarts from scratch.

impact_analysis.cost_impact: 2-hour video job + failure = wasted 2h of GPU time, zero output
impact_analysis.user_experience: No recovery from transient failures; users lose work
impact_analysis.technical_debt: Error handling taxonomy shows E4 is missing (Stage failure checkpoint)

gaps.root_cause: AutoFlow workflows use LangGraph StateGraph (graph persistence in PostgreSQL) but lack job-level queuing. If a worker process dies mid-execution, in-flight jobs are lost.

gap_components.requirement: Python library for distributed job queuing (e.g., rq, huey, or RabbitMQ replacement for BullMQ)
Job classes for each video stage (script->audio, audio->segments, etc.) with retry logic
gap_components.current: Workflow nodes exist but no separate job model
gap_components.missing: Job class definitions + checkpoint resume logic

gap_components.requirement: Logic to resume job from last successful stage if worker crashes
gap_components.current: StateGraph checkpoints everything, but job queue doesn't use them
gap_components.missing: Integration between job queue and StateGraph checkpoints

gap_components.requirement: Persistent storage for intermediate stage outputs (script.json, audio.wav, segments.json, etc.)
gap_components.missing: Supabase Storage / R2 bucket configuration for artifacts

acceptance_criteria: [ ] Job queue library installed and tested with Redis
[ ] 5 job classes created (one per video stage) with retry defaults (3 retries, 60s backoff)
[ ] Checkpoint resume logic: if stage 3 fails, stage 3 restarts with stage 2 outputs as input
[ ] Intermediate artifacts stored to Supabase Storage with lifecycle cleanup (24h TTL)
[ ] E2E test: simulate GPU worker crash during stage 3, verify automatic resume
[ ] Cost tracking: log each stage execution time + model used to /var/log/autoflow-jobs.jsonl
blocks: Gap 2 (GPU Worker integration) — can't handle long-running jobs without checkpoints
Gap 3 (LLM-Router alignment) — depends on stable job execution
gaps.implementation_notes: Use RQ (simpler than Celery/Airflow) with Redis backend already running on VPS. Each job class extends rq.job.Job and defines stage-specific command. StateGraph node outputs become job results for next stage.

gaps.title: Desktop GPU Worker Integration for Avatar/Matting/Voice/Rendering
gaps.current_state: gpu_worker_api.py exists (FastAPI server for Desktop) but is NOT integrated into AutoFlow workflows. Workflows can only execute CPU-bound tasks (LLM inference, script generation). GPU tasks (avatar generation, video matting, voice synthesis) are blocked on disconnected Desktop.

impact_analysis.blast_radius: Avatar, video matting, voice synthesis, image generation workflows
impact_analysis.cost_impact: GPU resources on Desktop sit idle; video pipeline can't complete
impact_analysis.user_experience: Video workflows fail with 'GPU task not supported' error
impact_analysis.technical_debt: AutoFlow-AIOX topology is incomplete: VPS handles LLM, Desktop handles GPU, but no bridge

gaps.root_cause: Split-brain topology (VPS + Desktop) requires explicit job submission to Desktop. AutoFlow workflows were designed for VPS-only execution; they have no mechanism to delegate to remote GPU worker. Cloudflare Tunnel is not configured; WireGuard not set up.

gap_components.requirement: Desktop GPU worker must be reachable from VPS over secure tunnel
gap_components.current: Desktop is on local network; VPS is on VPS provider network
gap_components.missing: Cloudflare Tunnel daemon on Desktop OR WireGuard peer config

gap_components.requirement: Python library to submit jobs to Desktop GPU worker, poll status, download artifacts
gap_components.current: gpu_worker_api.py exists, but no client library
gap_components.missing: GpuWorkerClient class wrapping HTTP calls to /api/avatar/generate, /api/video/matting, etc.

gap_components.requirement: Nodes in video_workflow.py that delegate GPU tasks to Desktop instead of failing
gap_components.missing: New workflow nodes: await_gpu_task(task_type, payload), with circuit breaker for offline Desktop

gap_components.requirement: If Desktop is offline, GPU tasks fail gracefully with clear error message, workflows continue with fallback (e.g., text-only video, static image)
gap_components.missing: Circuit breaker + fallback handler in gpu_router.py or new gpu_fallback.py

gap_components.requirement: Automatic detection of Desktop availability; Paperclip ticket if Desktop offline for >5 minutes
gap_components.missing: Health check task in autoflow-monitor.service or separate systemd service

acceptance_criteria: [ ] Cloudflare Tunnel setup on Desktop (or WireGuard peer config) with stable tunnel name
[ ] GpuWorkerClient class created with timeout=30s, retry logic (3 retries, exponential backoff)
[ ] Video workflow node accepts GPU task delegation: await_gpu_task('avatar', {script: ..., avatar_id: ...})
[ ] Circuit breaker: if Desktop offline, GPU task fails with clear error, workflow logs to Paperclip ticket
[ ] Health check: every 60s, verify /health endpoint; if down 5+ min, create Paperclip ticket 'Desktop GPU Worker Offline'
[ ] E2E test: submit avatar generation job from VPS → Desktop, verify job execution + artifact download
[ ] Cost tracking: log GPU job (model, latency, artifact size) to /var/log/autoflow-gpu.jsonl
dependencies: Gap 1 (BullMQ) — GPU jobs need checkpointing for long-running tasks
blocks: Gap 3 (LLM-Router alignment) — GPU task decision-making depends on stable architecture

effort_estimate.breakdown: 2h tunnel setup, 3h client library, 4h workflow integration, 2h testing, 1h documentation

gaps.implementation_notes: Defer avatar/matting/voice features until Desktop tunnel is stable. Start with health check + graceful fallback. GPU task requests go: VPS workflow -> GpuWorkerClient -> POST /api/{task_type} -> Desktop queues job -> VPS polls /api/jobs/{id} -> VPS downloads artifact from /api/jobs/{id}/artifact.

gaps.title: LLM-Router-AIOX Integration for Complexity-Based Model Selection
gaps.current_state: router.py has LLM-Router-AIOX client stub (_fetch_routing_decision) but doesn't use it for actual routing. Workflows use direct Ollama→Claude fallback chain, bypassing complexity scoring. LLM-Router-AIOX (located in /root/llm-router-aiox) exists with full feature set (complexity 1-15, circuit breaker, cost tracking, cache) but AutoFlow doesn't leverage it.

impact_analysis.blast_radius: All LLM calls from AutoFlow workflows
impact_analysis.cost_impact: Wrong model selection: simple queries hitting Claude (expensive) instead of Ollama (free). No cost tracking across AutoFlow.
impact_analysis.user_experience: No visibility into model selection logic; unpredictable latency
impact_analysis.technical_debt: Two separate routing systems: LLM-Router-AIOX (sophisticated) + AutoFlow router (simple). Inconsistent decisions.

gaps.root_cause: Architecture decision at Gap 1 of autoflow_execution_status.md: 'LLM-Router-AIOX is available but skipped for now (its API needs alignment).' The router.py code has a comment: 'Replace direct router with LLM-Router-AIOX API calls' but this was deferred. Root cause unclear — likely API signature mismatch or missing documentation.

gap_components.requirement: Document exact LLM-Router-AIOX API contract: request format, response format, error codes
gap_components.current: Code exists but no clear interface spec
gap_components.missing: .md file explaining /route endpoint: input (prompt, context, system), output (model, complexity_score, cost_estimate)

gap_components.requirement: Ensure AutoFlow prompts match LLM-Router-AIOX complexity scorer expectations
gap_components.current: router.py _fetch_routing_decision() is stubbed but never called
gap_components.missing: Test calls to LLM-Router-AIOX /route endpoint with sample AutoFlow prompts

gap_components.requirement: Replace Ollama→Claude direct fallback with LLM-Router-AIOX decision
gap_components.current: def call_llm_sync(prompt, system, ...) uses: try ollama -> except: claude
gap_components.missing: Sequence: LLM-Router /route decision -> execute on chosen model -> log cost

gap_components.requirement: Every LLM call logs: model, complexity_score, latency, cost to /var/log/autoflow-router.jsonl
gap_components.current: Logging infrastructure exists but not connected to LLM-Router outputs
gap_components.missing: Structured log format: {timestamp, workflow, prompt_hash, model, score, latency_ms, cost_usd}

gap_components.requirement: If LLM-Router-AIOX is down, fall back to direct Ollama→Claude, but don't call LLM-Router again for 5 min
gap_components.current: CircuitBreaker class exists in router.py but _fetch_routing_decision is never triggered
gap_components.missing: Wire CircuitBreaker into call_llm_sync's control flow

acceptance_criteria: [ ] Document LLM-Router-AIOX /route API endpoint (input/output contract) in /root/llm-router-aiox/API.md
[ ] Test _fetch_routing_decision() with sample AutoFlow prompts; verify response contains {model, complexity_score, cost_estimate}
[ ] Update call_llm_sync() to: (1) call LLM-Router /route, (2) execute on chosen model, (3) log result
[ ] Structured logging: every call writes {workflow, complexity_score, model, latency, cost} to /var/log/autoflow-router.jsonl
[ ] Circuit breaker: when /route fails, set 5min backoff; direct fallback without repeated calls
[ ] Dashboard query: SELECT SUM(cost_usd) FROM autoflow_router_logs WHERE workflow='video' — should show total cost per workflow
[ ] E2E test: trace a video workflow call through LLM-Router decision → model execution → cost log
dependencies: Gap 1 (BullMQ) — Job queue provides structured context for complexity scoring

effort_estimate.breakdown: 1h API documentation, 2h integration work, 2h testing, 1h dashboarding

gaps.implementation_notes: This is a refactoring, not new code. The pieces exist but are disconnected. Start by running a traced call: log_entry = trace_llm_call(prompt) -> fetch_routing_decision(prompt) -> call_model(model) -> log cost. Verify CircuitBreaker handles LLM-Router downtime gracefully.

gap_1.reason: Long-running video jobs need checkpointing before GPU integration; stable job execution enables complexity scoring

gap_2.reason: Desktop GPU worker can't handle job failures without checkpoint/resume from Gap 1

gap_3.reason: LLM-Router decision-making needs reliable job execution context from Gap 1

sequencing_recommendation.phase_1_critical: Gap 1 (BullMQ) — Foundation for both GPU and LLM-Router
sequencing_recommendation.rationale: Gap 1 unblocks Gap 2 and Gap 3 independently. Once Gap 1 is complete, Gap 2 and Gap 3 can proceed in parallel (GPU handles infrastructure, LLM-Router handles control flow).

rollout_timeline.sprint_1_days_1_3: Gap 1 — BullMQ job queue + checkpointing (8 hours, 1 sprint)
rollout_timeline.sprint_1_days_4_5: Gap 3 — LLM-Router alignment + cost tracking (6 hours, 0.75 sprint)
rollout_timeline.sprint_2_days_1_5: Gap 2 — GPU Worker integration + tunnel setup (12 hours, 1.5 sprints)
rollout_timeline.total_effort: 26 hours (3.25 sprints, ~5-6 working days for full team)

gap_2_risk.title: Desktop availability variance
gap_2_risk.impact: GPU tasks fail silently if Desktop offline during business hours
gap_2_risk.mitigation: Health check + Paperclip alerting (part of acceptance criteria). Consider fallback rendering on VPS (CPU, slower).

gap_3_risk.title: LLM-Router API signature drift
gap_3_risk.impact: Integration breaks if LLM-Router changes /route response format
gap_3_risk.mitigation: Document API contract in LLM-Router repo. Add integration tests. Version API (/v1/route, /v2/route).

success_metrics.gap_1: Video pipeline survives 5+ random worker crashes with <30s recovery time per crash
success_metrics.gap_2: Avatar generation requests succeed 99% of time (1% fail = Desktop offline); fallback graceful
success_metrics.gap_3: Cost tracking dashboard shows ±5% of actual API spend; complexity score distribution makes sense (simple=ollama, complex=claude)
